publishedAbstract: This particular issue is related to multipath being enabled storage nodes after ODF had already been configured with the Local Storage Operator which resulted in a conflict over the block device between multipathd and ODF. In standard multipath/ODF deployments, multipath is configured first, the volumes provisioned from that multipath can then be used by the Local Storage Operator to pick up and present as `Available` PVs for consumption to ODF. ~~~ NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE rook-ceph-osd-1-d99d97749-nzcbh 2/2 Running 0 3d rook-ceph-osd-11-5954d48f5f-qmqsf 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 220 18h rook-ceph-osd-12-7447bf8fbb-b9fsm 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 240 20h rook-ceph-osd-13-845f5c6c68-9pkcv 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 225 19h rook-ceph-osd-14-58cf45c9d6-rhrm4 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 221 18h rook-ceph-osd-15-6495cfcd76-dnccw 0/2 Init:Error 231 19h rook-ceph-osd-16-6b5f74847d-q97r4 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 228 19h rook-ceph-osd-17-6df5947958-n5dbw 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 234 19h rook-ceph-osd-18-58b9f5b887-9sspk 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 219 18h rook-ceph-osd-19-79fc468b95-hx4fh 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 236 20h rook-ceph-osd-2-8489f9579b-pjrgw 2/2 Running 0 3d rook-ceph-osd-20-6d9c468bb5-9zcfh 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 224 18h rook-ceph-osd-21-7d75589c65-6lmsh 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 242 20h rook-ceph-osd-3-5c748d78d9-j8qr6 2/2 Running 0 3d rook-ceph-osd-4-5c7956975d-52s4z 2/2 Running 0 3d rook-ceph-osd-5-5f496c67d5-cckdx 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 1064 3d rook-ceph-osd-6-584d44f4df-5rpwp 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 1055 3d rook-ceph-osd-7-6cd54cb4-hgq64 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 13 1h rook-ceph-osd-8-57d95dfd6c-zx8ks 0/2 Init:CrashLoopBackOff 255 21h rook-ceph-osd-9-7bcdcb5dc6-x6t8k 0/2 Init:2/4 1063 3d ~~~ The expand-bluefs container is simply the container that failed once the device could not be allocated. Observing the expand-bluefs container failure in the events doesn't necessarily mean you're facing this particular issue, however, this solution would be good to review and validate multipathd is not interfering with the allocation of the block device to ODF. If multipathd and particularly the error of: `Device or resource busy` is not affecting your environment, below are some related articles with `expand-bluefs` container errors on the OSD devices. [ODF 4.X- OSD pod Init:CrashLoopBackOff due to initContainer expand-bluefs- BlueStore::expand_devices](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7014699) [ODF/Ceph: OSD Pods are in CrashLoopBackOff (CLBO) after ODF 4.14.x upgrade.](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7063703)
publishedTitle: mulltipath (mpath) causes Local Storage Operator (LSO) Devices "Back-off restarting failed container expand-bluefs in pod rook-ceph-osd-<id>" "Device or resource busy" CLBO - OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF)
